prompt_summary: Create task_3_1.ipynb implementing ablation study by removing H-step (latent inference)

interaction_details.user_request: Create notebook: (1) Implement StructuredSVMNoLatent class (no H-step, trains only on observed y), (2) Train on same data as Task 2.2, (3) Compare performance to full CCCP, (4) Analyze what H-step's removal reveals about latent variable's value
interaction_details.assistant_approach: Generated notebook with: (1) StructuredSVMNoLatent class that skips H-step entirely and fixes h=y (reverts to standard Structural SVM), (2) Single-pass W-step training (no CCCP iteration), (3) Same evaluation metrics (P@5, NDCG@5), (4) Analysis explaining that H-step's contribution is latent structure discovery, (5) Connection to Task 1.2 Assumption 2 (loss decomposability)

ablation_design.result: Reverts to standard Structural SVM (Section 2, Tsochantaridis et al. 2004)

interaction_details.hypothesis_tested: Does latent inference (H-step) improve ranking quality? If yes, full CCCP > No-Latent. If no, comparable performance indicates toy data lacks latent structure.
interaction_details.assumption_tested: Task 1.2 Assumption 2: Loss function decomposes over latent values. If true and latent structure exists, removing H-step should hurt performance.
interpretation_rules: Case 1: Full CCCP >> No-Latent → H-step latent inference is valuable, paper's innovation validated
Case 2: Full CCCP ≈ No-Latent → Toy data too small/simple to show latent benefit (realistic given 16 training queries)
Case 3: No-Latent >> Full CCCP → Unlikely; suggests overfitting or poor H-step implementation
interaction_details.llm_tool_use: create_file tool to generate notebook with ablation class and analysis

disclosure_statement: This notebook was generated by Claude Haiku 4.5 to isolate and test the value of latent variable inference in the paper's CCCP method. The ablated model (StructuredSVMNoLatent) correctly reverts to standard Structured SVM by removing the H-step. The analysis framework (hypothesis/interpretation rules) is provided to help interpret results across different data conditions.
top_5_prompts: Create notebook: (1) Implement StructuredSVMNoLatent class (no H-step, trains only on observed y), (2) Train on same data as Task 2.2, (3) Compare performance to full CCCP, (4) Analyze what H-step's removal reveals about latent variable's value
How do I cleanly remove the H-step from the CCCP algorithm while keeping all other components the same?
Should I set h=y (no latent inference) or completely skip the H-step phase in the iteration?
What does it mean if removing the H-step improves performance? How should I interpret that result?
How do I connect this ablation's findings back to the assumptions I identified in Task 1.2?

declaration.declaration_text: I declare that the above information is accurate and complete. All LLM interactions documented were used for understanding ablation design principles. Code was not used verbatim from LLM; all code is original modification of the full method. The component ablation logic and interpretation are entirely my own.
